docs.title: Kokoro Tech
docs.text:
Kokoro Tech is a small engineering studio building crypto and fintech tooling. This site documents projects that have been individually reviewed and confirmed to exist in a meaningful state.

All projects listed here are under active development. None have been validated for production use. Do not treat any of this as stable software.

The organization has other repositories that are not yet reviewed or verified. Those projects are not documented here. Only the six projects below appear on this site.

docs.title: Kokoro Terminal
docs.text: A WebGPU-based terminal for viewing crypto market data. This is the project under active development.

docs.title: Kokoro Auth
docs.text: A unified identity provider for the Kokoro ecosystem, covering SSO, JWT authentication, and per-product subscription management.

docs.title: Kokoro VPN
docs.text: A self-hosted WireGuard VPN platform supporting both hub-and-spoke client VPN and mesh VPN configurations.

docs.title: Kokoro Pay
docs.text: A multi-chain stablecoin payment processor targeting USDC and USDT, with treasury management and invoicing capabilities.

docs.title: Kokoro Constitution
docs.text: An engineering governance framework that defines standards, processes, and rules for all Kokoro Tech engineering work.

docs.title: Kokoro Claude Skills
docs.text: A collection of Claude Code skills that automate parts of the constitutional governance process.

docs.title: About this site
docs.text:
This documentation is maintained as a public record of verified work. It is intentionally narrow in scope. Pages are only added when a project has been individually confirmed. Content is written to be accurate and understated — if something is uncertain, it is either omitted or stated as uncertain.

Kokoro Terminal is the current focus of active development. The other five projects exist in various states of early development and are documented here for organizational clarity, not as an indication that they are ready for use.

docs.title: Kokoro Auth
docs.text:
Repository: https://github.com/happykokoro/kokoro-auth

Kokoro Auth is the central identity provider for the Kokoro ecosystem. Its purpose is to provide a single authentication layer across all Kokoro products, so that each product does not need to implement its own auth.

docs.title: What it aims to do
docs.text:
• Single sign-on (SSO) across Kokoro products
  • JWT-based authentication
  • Per-product subscription management, so that a user identity can carry different access levels for different services

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Under development. Not validated for production use. The scope and implementation details are not finalized.

docs.title: Kokoro Claude Skills
docs.text:
Repository: https://github.com/happykokoro/kokoro-claude-skills

Kokoro Claude Skills is a collection of Claude Code skills that automate parts of the governance process defined in the Kokoro Constitution. The skills are invoked within Claude Code sessions to run structured audits and checks without requiring manual execution of each step.

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
Wiring audit — Audits the wiring of a codebase or component against expected patterns or contracts. Surfaces discrepancies between what is documented and what is implemented.

Disclosure audit — Reviews code or configuration for items that should be disclosed, flagged, or documented according to governance requirements.

Constitution check — Checks work against the rules and standards in the Kokoro Constitution. Used to verify that a changeset or proposal is consistent with current governance policy.

docs.title: Relationship to other projects
docs.text: These skills operationalize rules defined in the Kokoro Constitution. They are tools for enforcing the governance framework, not a replacement for it.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Under development. The skill definitions and the governance processes they implement are both subject to change as the constitution evolves.

docs.title: Kokoro Constitution
docs.text:
Repository: https://github.com/happykokoro/kokoro-constitution

Kokoro Constitution is the engineering governance framework for Kokoro Tech. It defines the standards, processes, and rules that apply to all engineering work across the organization.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Engineering teams accumulate informal norms over time. The constitution externalizes those norms into a document that can be referenced, debated, and amended. This makes governance explicit rather than implicit.

The framework covers areas such as:

  • Engineering standards and conventions
  • Development processes
  • Review and audit requirements
  • Rules for how the framework itself can be changed

docs.title: Relationship to other projects
docs.text: The Kokoro Claude Skills project implements automation for some of the audit and review processes defined here. The constitution is the policy layer; the skills are part of the tooling layer.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Under development. The framework is not finalized. Content and scope are subject to change.

docs.title: Kokoro Pay
docs.text:
Repository: https://github.com/happykokoro/kokoro-pay

Kokoro Pay is a multi-chain stablecoin payment processor. It is designed to accept payments in USDC and USDT across chains, and to handle the downstream responsibilities that come with receiving on-chain payments in a business context.

docs.title: What it aims to do
docs.text:
• Accept USDC and USDT payments across supported chains
  • Treasury management: tracking inflows, routing funds, maintaining balances
  • Invoicing: generating and tracking payment requests

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Under development. Not validated for production use. Chain support, treasury logic, and invoicing features are in early stages.

docs.title: Kokoro Terminal
docs.text:
Repository: https://github.com/happykokoro/kokoro-terminal

Kokoro Terminal is a WebGPU-based terminal for displaying and interacting with crypto market data. It is the primary active project at Kokoro Tech and is currently under development.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
The project is organized as a Bun monorepo with four main components:

frontend-components — A library of UI components and rendering logic. This is where the WebGPU rendering work lives, along with the core data visualization primitives used by the terminal.

apps/api-gateway — The API gateway that sits between the frontend and upstream data sources. Handles routing, authentication, and aggregation.

apps/data-ingest — The data ingestion service responsible for pulling market data from external sources and making it available to the rest of the system.

apps/dev-server — A local development server for running the frontend during development.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Under active development. The codebase is changing frequently. It is not ready for use by anyone outside the team.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: This is the only Kokoro Tech project currently receiving regular commits. Documentation for this project will expand as the architecture stabilizes.

docs.title: Kokoro VPN
docs.text:
Repository: https://github.com/happykokoro/kokoro-vpn

Kokoro VPN is a self-hosted VPN platform built on WireGuard. The goal is to provide a manageable VPN infrastructure that supports two distinct network topologies without requiring a separate tool for each.

docs.title: Modes
docs.text:
Hub-and-spoke (client VPN) — Clients connect to a central gateway. The standard pattern for corporate VPN access where remote devices reach a private network through a central point.

Mesh VPN — Peers connect directly to each other without routing through a central gateway. Suitable for low-latency communication between nodes in a distributed system.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
The platform consists of three components:

  • A Rust/Axum API backend that manages peers, configuration, and keys
  • A Tauri-based desktop client for end-user connection management
  • A CLI tool for scripted or headless operation

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Under development. Not validated for production use. The three components are at different stages of completion.
